name: Italian
blurb: Standard Italian, the italiano of school, television and the state offices, with no regional dialect mixed in. Corlang takes you to B1, because B1 is exactly what Italian citizenship by residence or by marriage requires, and it is the level the CILS and CELI certificates accepted by the Ministero dell'Interno are built around. There is no A0 onramp here: Italian spelling maps onto sound so regularly that you can read aloud from the first lesson, so the course starts at A1 and spends its time on grammar and vocabulary instead.
paretoSummary: 80% of everyday Italian comes from a small core. Learn the present tense of the three conjugations (-are, -ere, -ire, plus the -isc- verbs like capire) and the two verbs everything else hangs from, essere and avere. Learn that every noun is masculine or feminine, singular or plural, and that the article and every adjective must agree with it, so gender is not decoration but the thing that makes a sentence hold together. Learn the articles il, lo, la, i, gli, le and the way di, a, da, in and su fuse with them into del, alla, dal, nel and sui, because those fused forms appear in almost every sentence you will ever hear. Learn the passato prossimo with its two auxiliaries, avere for most verbs and essere for movement, change of state and all reflexives, and the participle agreement that follows from the choice. Learn the imperfetto for description, habit and background, and the moment you can pick between ho fatto and facevo you can tell a story. Learn the direct and indirect object pronouns and where they sit, before a conjugated verb and attached to the end of an infinitive or an imperative. And listen for double consonants, because they are meaningful: fato is fate, fatto is done, and nono is ninth while nonno is grandfather. Master this 20% and Italy opens up.
